title: Hive Song Practice
desc: Every voice matters to the hive. Even the ones that can't hold a note.

steps.text:
Hive Song is Danian Mugic in its rawest form — not the refined spells of individual muges, but the communal resonance of hundreds of voices synchronized into a single harmonic. When the hive sings together, the walls vibrate, the air thickens, and the Mugic becomes structural — literally reinforcing Mount Pillar's foundations.

Today is mandatory rehearsal. Every Danian in this sector is expected to attend. The choirmaster — a muge whose voice alone can crack stone — stands on the conducting platform with an expression that suggests she has opinions about attendance.

"Section twelve, you're flat. Section nine, you're sharp. Section fifteen, you're not here, which is worse than being flat." She taps her baton. "From the beginning. The Pillar Resonance. You all know it. Some of you will pretend you don't. Sing anyway."

options.label: Sing — you know the Pillar Resonance
Observe from the balcony — study the harmonics

steps.text:
The Pillar Resonance begins low — a bass note so deep you feel it in your chest before you hear it. You join section twelve, finding your pitch in the mass of voices around you. The note builds. Adjacent sections add higher harmonics. The sound layers, each voice contributing a frequency that interlocks with the others.

When it works, it's extraordinary. The air solidifies with sound. The walls hum in sympathy. You can feel the Mugic building — not a spell cast by a single muge, but a collective force generated by the hive's unified voice.

When it doesn't work, it's painful. Section nine drifts sharp, creating a dissonance that feels like pressure behind your eyes. The choirmaster stops the choir with a raised hand.

"Section nine. Your anchor is drifting. Find the bass note. Lock to it. You are not soloists. You are part of a wall." She demonstrates — a single note, pure, unwavering. Section nine follows. The dissonance resolves.

They restart. This time, the Resonance builds clean — bass to tenor to treble, each section adding its layer. The Grand Hall vibrates. Dust falls from the ceiling in gentle streams. The Mugic reaches critical resonance and the walls pulse once — a deep, subsonic throb that passes through every body in the room.

The choirmaster lowers her baton. "Better. Tomorrow, the Hive Chord. It has twelve parts. Practice."

You leave the hall with your bones still vibrating.

options.label: Hum the Pillar Resonance on the way home

steps.text:
The balcony offers a view of the entire choir — three hundred Danians arranged in concentric sections, each section responsible for a different harmonic. From above, you can see the sound as much as hear it. The air ripples above each section like heat shimmer, the frequencies visible in the way dust motes dance.

The choirmaster conducts with precise gestures — each hand movement directing a section, adjusting volume, shifting pitch. It's architecture expressed as music, and watching her work is like watching a builder construct something invisible.

You notice something the singers can't see from floor level: the harmonics have a physical pattern. When the Resonance is working, the ripples from each section align — overlapping, reinforcing, creating standing waves that pulse outward from the center. When a section drifts off-key, the waves break, creating turbulence in the air above.

You sketch the wave patterns. The choirmaster spots you after the rehearsal and examines your sketches.

"These are good. I conduct by ear, but I've always suspected the harmonics have visual structure." She holds up your sketch of the correct alignment. "This is what I'm trying to build. Can you come to every rehearsal? Watch from up here, tell me which sections are breaking the wave pattern?"

You've accidentally become the hive's acoustic spotter. It's not what you planned, but the choirmaster's grin suggests you don't have a choice.

options.label: Accept the new role as acoustic spotter
